verdicts.note: Lower taxes support the direction, but no maximal tax-cut mechanism is documented.
Public safety is only named as a priority; the dossier gives no enforcement-versus-prevention mechanism.
The absence of an elected record supports a strong outsider placement, but the sparse background warrants medium rather than high confidence.
Public investment and service-delivery priorities support expansion, but the dossier lacks a comprehensive maximal program.
Homelessness prevention and behavioral health are documented priorities, but the dossier does not frame them as a public-safety approach.
Nonprofit executive and service-delivery leadership provide meaningful institutional experience despite no elected record.
City-council and deputy-mayor service establish a strong elected governing record.
State legislative and justice-policy work provide institutional experience, while the absence of elected tenure supports a genuinely mixed placement.
Accessible services and education priorities do not establish an overall spending posture without a funding or expansion mechanism.
State policy-office experience balances the absence of elected tenure.

review_notes: All 14 original candidate-axis pairs were reviewed exactly once (7 adjust, 4 upheld, 3 refuted). No omitted axis had sufficiently specific dossier evidence for a supported missing proposal.
